name: La Vall de Laguar — Circular desde Pego por el Barranco del Infierno y Pla de Petracos
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Pego, located in the Marina Alta region of Alicante, Spain, offer diverse terrain for riders. The area is characterized by surrounding mountain ranges such as the Sierra de Segaria and Serra de Mostalla, providing challenging climbs and technical descents. The Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park features paths through wetlands and rice fields, offering more relaxed cycling experiences. This landscape combines rugged mountains with scenic valleys and natural parks. Best mountain bike trails around Pego The most popular mountain bike trail is La Vall de Laguar — Circular desde Pego por el Barranco del Infierno y Pla de Petracos, a 31.2 miles (50.3 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos MTB Trail, a difficult 23.7 miles (38.2 km) path. This route navigates through valleys and offers varied terrain, including sections with rocky surfaces. Local mountain bikers also love the La Vall d'Ebo – Vall d'Ebo Pass loop from Pego, a 28.7 miles (46.1 km) trail leading through the Vall d'Ebo Pass, often completed in about 4 hours 0 minutes. Mountain biking around Pego is defined by rugged mountains, scenic valleys, and wetland paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from technical singletrack to more accessible routes. The routes in Pego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Pego?
A: Pego offers a wide network of mountain bike trails. Our guide features 12 distinct routes, ranging from easy paths through wetlands to challenging mountain ascents. The region as a whole boasts hundreds of kilometers of trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Pego's mountain bike trails?
A: You'll find a diverse range of terrain. The mountainous areas, like the Sierra de Segaria and Serra de Mostalla, feature challenging climbs, technical descents, and often rocky, steep, and loose surfaces. In contrast, the Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park offers more relaxed cycling through wetlands, meadows, and rice fields.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Pego?
A: Yes, while many trails are difficult, there are options for beginners. The Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park, for instance, has easy biking routes, such as the one leading to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Salty Spring&lt;/a&gt;, which provide a more relaxed experience amidst unique flora and fauna.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Pego?
A: Yes, many routes in Pego are designed as loops. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926019925/la-vall-de-laguar-circular-desde-pego-por-el-barranco-del-infierno-y-pla-de-petracos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vall de Laguar — Circular desde Pego por el Barranco del Infierno y Pla de Petracos&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult circular trail, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14691364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vall d'Ebo – Vall d'Ebo Pass loop from Pego&lt;/a&gt; also offers a challenging circular experience.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural attractions to see while mountain biking in Pego?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2916765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Cocoll&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5407844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; with its rice fields and wetlands. Some routes also pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/276237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Skulls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Pego a good destination for mountain biking with children or family?
A: Pego offers options for families, particularly in the Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park, where paths are generally flatter and less technical. These routes provide a relaxed cycling experience suitable for various ages, allowing families to enjoy the unique natural environment together.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly mountain bike trails in Pego?
A: While specific trails are not designated as 'dog-friendly,' many natural areas and less technical paths in Pego are suitable for riding with well-behaved dogs on a leash. Always ensure your dog is fit for the distance and terrain, and carry enough water for both of you.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Pego?
A: Pego generally experiences sunny and warm weather for much of the year, making it conducive for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn are often ideal, offering pleasant temperatures. Even in summer, early mornings or late afternoons can be suitable, though it's important to stay hydrated and avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there facilities like bike rentals or support services in Pego?
A: Yes, Pego is equipped with facilities to enhance your biking experience. Services like Pego.CC offer bike rental, maintenance, and professional support, including authorized test centers for brands like Giant and Liv. This makes it convenient for visitors to access suitable equipment and assistance.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Pego?
A: The mountain bike trails in Pego are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic wetland paths, and the stunning panoramic views of the valleys and the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Pego?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Pego, particularly in the town center and at common starting points for popular routes. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check local maps or route descriptions for designated parking areas to ensure convenient access.

Q: Are there any trails that offer particularly stunning views?
A: Absolutely. Many routes through the mountainous areas provide breathtaking panoramic views. You can expect to see the surrounding valleys, the Mediterranean Sea, and the expansive Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park from various vantage points, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2916765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Cocoll&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some of the more challenging mountain bike trails in Pego?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926019925/la-vall-de-laguar-circular-desde-pego-por-el-barranco-del-infierno-y-pla-de-petracos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vall de Laguar — Circular desde Pego por el Barranco del Infierno y Pla de Petracos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e925804960/vall-d-alcala-pla-de-petracos-mtb-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vall d'Alcalà Pla de Petracos MTB Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and technical sections. These routes navigate rugged mountain terrain with rocky surfaces and demanding descents.
tips: Panoramic point towards the Natural Park of La Marjal de Pego-Oliva

Very striking rock formation on the banks of the Rio Girona ravine and a few meters from the Barranc de l'Infern. The site draws attention for its immense inclined rock wall in which the strata of the geological process can be seen.

Downstream section of the Barranc de l'Infern, an icon of the Marina Alta area. It is reached by the path that goes to the d'Isbert dam and is notable for its high rocky wall. A very peculiar place with a lot of charm. To get to the dam you must dismount the bike at the end of the paved road and get there on foot.
Ruins of old Polvorí located in a very picturesque setting with beautiful views of the valley.
Panoramic point towards the town of Campell and the valley of Vall de Laguart. Beautiful views and marks the beginning of a panoramic path on the side of the road.

Town belonging to the municipality of La Vall de Laguart. It is a very quiet and picturesque place with beautiful views of the coast and many trails to explore the beautiful valley. A highly recommended place!
You have to be very prepared
A wonderful site with amazing views! From here appreciate the views and then continue to the Coll de Garga where there is a restaurant and recreational area. A highly recommended site!

The ascent from Sagra is done along the provincial road, it is quite calm. Halfway up the climb, in the town of Benimaurell, the road becomes a paved road. Above all, the locals use it to move to their crops. There are steps to overtaking. The last slopes are very steep, save your energy for when you get to this section.

At the end of the climb, there is a restaurant on the right hand side. It is an ideal place to try typical foods of the mountains.

The Pla de Petracos area has been inhabited since prehistoric times and there is one of the best cave art sites. The archaeological site of Pla de Petracos is located in the area of its name about 5 km north of Castell de Castells and is accessed by paved road.
Very striking and picturesque site on the Camí de Tarrenyes and a few meters from the archaeological site of Pla de Petracos.
Paved road with beautiful views of the Vall d'Ebo. Take the detour a few meters from the town of Vall d'Ebo and go up to the Caseta del Collado de Les Foites. A very picturesque road!

Amazing path with incredible views towards the sea and the Natural Park of La Marjal Pego-Oliva. I recommend doing it descending from the Caseta del Collado de Les Foites since you can better appreciate the panoramic views and enjoy the descent. In every corner the views are more spectacular!

waypoints.name: Steep climb to Venta Del Collao/Back way to Vall de Laguar
